title: MANGA NGE The manga as a reference
display_title: MANGA NGE The manga as a reference
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] The manga as a reference.             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 27, 2006 6:20 pm There are already numerous differences between the Evangelion anime series, and the unfinished manga. These include plot differences, character personalities, character relationships and even the absence of many of the angels. In the latest volumes the manga has begun to diverge considerably from the series, and their remains a very open question as to how the manga will portray the finale. At this point, I would like to ask; What are people's opinions on the relationship between the anime and the manga? Are they closely related, or is the manga only distantly connected? Can ideas, concepts and themes in the manga be applied to interpretation and criticism of the anime? How applicable is the manga as a reference for the anime?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 27, 2006 6:51 pm I would say only distantly related - and take the manga as suggetive of what might fill in gaps where the anime is completely silent e.g. when Ritsuko was accosted by the MiBs

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jan 28, 2006 2:34 am I would never used the manga as a source to fill in any gaps with the anime and vice versa. They are separate continuities, separate works, and further more should be evaluated by their own merits, not by a precedant set by one another.

[]:             Quote Post by Fallen angel » Sat Jul 01, 2006 1:57 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I would never used the manga as a source to fill in any gaps with the anime and vice versa. They are separate continuities, separate works, and further more should be evaluated by their own merits, not by a precedant set by one another.

Article: MANGA NGE The manga as a reference
[]: [NGE] The manga as a reference.             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 27, 2006 6:20 pm There are already numerous differences between the Evangelion anime series, and the unfinished manga. These include plot differences, character personalities, character relationships and even the absence of many of the angels. In the latest volumes the manga has begun to diverge considerably from the series, and their remains a very open question as to how the manga will portray the finale. At this point, I would like to ask; What are people's opinions on the relationship between the anime and the manga? Are they closely related, or is the manga only distantly connected? Can ideas, concepts and themes in the manga be applied to interpretation and criticism of the anime? How applicable is the manga as a reference for the anime?
